The classification of Palicourea cypellantha reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants within its family.

intro: Palicourea cypellantha, also known as Copaiba, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and studies have shown that it offers potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as possible antiviral and anticancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Copaiba include sesquiterpenes, particularly β-caryophyllene and α- and β-caryophyllene oxides, which have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Copaiba include oils, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine for their potential therapeutic benefits.
